data.title: China considers aggressive new stimulus to rescue property sector amid financial system fears - report
data.description:
Chinese policymakers are reportedly considering a new, aggressive package of measures aimed at reviving their troubled housing market, as fears mount that the long crisis could now threaten the entire financial system, Bloomberg reported, citing sources familiar with the discussions.

As per the report, the urgency for new intervention is underscored by the deepening stress on the banking sector. The property crisis, combined with the weakened ability of households to service their debt, has led to warnings about deteriorating bank asset quality.

Officials, including those within the Housing Ministry, are exploring a range of options, including providing mortgage subsidies to new homebuyers nationwide (a first for national policy), raising income tax rebates for existing mortgage borrowers, and reducing the costs associated with home transactions, one of the people said.

The planned policies have been under discussion since at least the third quarter; however, the timing and specific details of implementation remain uncertain.

China's housing market remains trapped in a deep, four-year downturn that continues to weigh heavily on the national economy. The crisis has had a widespread negative impact, directly depressing domestic consumption, employment, and overall household wealth.

While earlier government support briefly stabilized the sector, that momentum quickly faded, with recent data showing home sales declining since the second quarter and fixed-asset investment collapsing last month.

Fitch Ratings analysts issued a warning last month that Chinese banks’ asset quality could deteriorate further next year. This concern follows a surge in non-performing loans, which reached a record high of 3.5 trillion yuan (US$492 billion) by the end of September.

The worsening outlook for the property market, combined with a diminished capacity for households to repay mortgages and personal loans, poses a serious risk to the financial sector.

In an effort to stabilize the sector and mitigate this systemic risk, the government's plan to subsidise new mortgages is intended to lure reluctant homebuyers back into the free-falling market and stabilize prices.

On Thursday, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.2% to above 3,950 while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.15% to 13,060 on Thursday, with mainland stocks showing mixed performances after China’s central bank kept key lending rates unchanged as expected, and the offshore yuan steadied around 7.11 per dollar on Thursday.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSE:TDF), (NYSEARCA:CHIQ), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:KBA), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:CWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:KURE).

Currency: (CNY:USD)

data.title: Meta's chief AI scientist Yann LeCun announces departure to start his own firm
data.description:
Meta's (META) chief AI scientist and the founding director of the FAIR lab, Yann LeCun, is leaving the company at the end of 2025, according to an official memo posted on his LinkedIn account late on Wednesday. 

LeCun, who has been with the social media and tech giant for 12 years, said he plans to start a new company and will partner with Meta and give it access to its innovations. 

In his public post, LeCun said he is going to create a startup company to continue the "Advanced Machine Intelligence" research program he has been pursuing over the last several years with colleagues at FAIR, at New York University, and other places.

"The goal of the startup is to bring about the next big revolution in AI: systems that understand the physical world, have persistent memory, can reason, and can plan complex action sequences," LeCun said.

The announcement of LeCun's departure also comes amid a major C-suite rejig at Meta, which saw the exit of revenue chief John Hegeman and Clara Shih, the head of Business AI.

LeCun's job at the Mark Zuckerberg-led company focused on long-term AI research, which won’t play a part in shaping consumer experiences in the near future. According to sources who spoke to Bloomberg, LeCun had difficulty getting resources for his projects as Meta focused more intently on building models to compete with immediate threats from rivals including OpenAI (OPENAI), Google (GOOG) (GOOGL), and Anthropic PBC (ANTHRO).

After concluding Meta had fallen behind key rivals, Zuckerberg shifted his focus away from the research efforts of FAIR and toward faster deployment of AI models and products. Over the summer, he brought in Alexandr Wang to run a new "superintelligence" division, spending $14.3B to hire the 28-year-old Scale AI founder and acquire a 49% stake in his company.

In October, the company laid off 600 employees in its AI division but retained top talent to increase efficiency and offset the impact of its aggressive capital spending on building critical AI infrastructure.

data.title: ZTO adjusts 2025 parcel volume guidance to 38.2B-38.7B amid industry shift to quality competition
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: ZTO Express (Cayman) Inc. (ZTO) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Meisong Lai reported that ZTO grew parcel volume by 9.8% year-over-year to 9.57 billion parcels and achieved adjusted net income of CNY 2.51 billion, up 5% from the prior year. Lai stated, "China's express delivery industry experienced steady growth during the third quarter of 2025. While maintaining its industry-leading service quality, ZTO grew its parcel volume by 9.8% year-over-year to reach 9.57 billion parcels. Our adjusted net income was CNY 2.51 billion, which rose 5% over the same period last year." The CEO emphasized the company's balanced approach to quality and growth, highlighting a focus on upgrading last-mile capabilities, optimizing network policies, and advancing cost efficiency through technology and digitization. Lai noted, "ZTO's retail parcel volume maintained strong growth momentum and grew close to 50% year-on-year. Through optimizing the pickup model and the refined lean process management, we enhanced both service quality and cost efficiency."

  • CFO Huiping Yan provided further detail: "Our parcel volume reached 9.6 billion, which grew 9.8%. Adjusted net income increased 5% to CNY 2.5 billion. ASP for core express delivery business increased 1.7% or CNY 0.02, and the breakdown are the following: CNY 0.18 positive contribution from increase in KA volume, mainly comprised of headquarter contracted reverse logistics products and services."

Outlook

  • CFO Huiping Yan adjusted ZTO's annual volume guidance downward to a range of 38.2 billion to 38.7 billion parcels, representing a year-over-year growth of 12.3% to 13.8%. Yan explained, "With visibility into the final quarter of the year, we are adjusting down the annual volume guidance to be in the range of 38.2 billion to 38.7 billion parcels, representing a year-over-year growth of 12.3% to 13.8%. Volume is critical to a scale-leveraged business and partner network stability is the foundation for sustainable long-term growth of our company."

  • Management cited moderating industry growth and ongoing macroeconomic uncertainties as reasons for the revised guidance and emphasized confidence in ZTO's strategy to address evolving industry dynamics.

Financial Results

  • CFO Huiping Yan reported, "Total revenue increased 11.1% to CNY 11.9 billion as a combined result of volume and price increase. Total cost of revenue was CNY 8.9 billion, which increased 21.4% as a blended result of significant increase in costs associated with non-ecommerce volume relative to the rate of decrease in cost for e-commerce volume."

  • The company reported a decrease in gross profit to CNY 3 billion and a gross margin rate of 24.9%. Yan added, "Income from operations decreased 15.4% to CNY 2.4 billion and associated margin dropped 6.3 percent -- points to 20.3%. Operating cash flow was CNY 3.2 billion for the quarter, representing a 3.2% increase. Adjusted EBITDA decreased 4.2% to CNY 3.6 billion. Capital expenditures for Q3 totaled CNY 1.2 billion, and we anticipate our annual CapEx expenses in 2025 to be CNY 5.5 billion to CNY 6 billion."

Q&A

  • Ronald Keung, Goldman Sachs: Asked about industry structure and ZTO's approach to integrated logistics. CFO Yan responded that focus remains on "scale and better services as well as higher efficiency, cost effectiveness," and outlined efforts to build comprehensive capabilities beyond express delivery, including LTL, cold chain, and warehouse cloud operations.

  • Qianlei Fan, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about the impact of anti-involution policies and regulatory engagement. CFO Yan stated, "Since August of this year, the anti-involution policy has been progressively rolled out across most regions nationwide, aiming at rational recovery in pricing... We expect this trend to continue and the effort will also continue to take effect." Yan explained that regulatory consultation aligned with industry objectives and was a catalyst for internal improvements.

  • Aaron Luo, UBS: Queried about the drivers of volume slowdown and future growth. CFO Yan explained, "The recent deceleration in the industry growth is primarily due to the price increase driven by the involution -- anti-involution policy. This adjustment where overall logistic price has increased and has a greater impact on low margin and highly price-sensitive e-commerce merchants, resulting in a decline in that segment of the parcel." Yan expects industry volume growth to stabilize around 10% next year.

  • Unknown Analyst: Sought clarity on cost reduction and competitive structure. CFO Yan described ongoing initiatives in technology and automation to maintain cost leadership and manage shifts in parcel mix.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed concerns about slowing industry volume, regulatory impacts, and cost pressures, with a neutral to slightly negative tone indicated by the focus on near-term challenges and policy changes.

  • Management maintained a confident but measured tone, highlighting strategic adaptability and operational strengths. Yan used phrases such as "we are confident in our ability to execute the overall corporate strategy as well as tackling challenges in the near term."

  • The tone in management's prepared remarks was more optimistic, while in Q&A responses, management was more detailed and acknowledged industry headwinds. Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management displayed a slightly more cautious stance due to industry moderation and guidance revisions.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter saw a reduction in annual parcel volume guidance from the previous range of 38.8-40.1 billion (Q2) to 38.2-38.7 billion (Q3), reflecting a more cautious outlook.

  • Management continued to emphasize high-quality growth and operational efficiency, but the strategic narrative shifted from expansion to navigating a more mature and regulated competitive environment.

  • Analysts' focus shifted from technology and AI implementation in Q2 to regulatory impacts, pricing, and volume slowdown in Q3.

  • Key metrics such as adjusted net income and operating margin declined from the previous quarter, while retail parcel volume continued robust growth.

  • Management sentiment was more cautious than in Q2, with increased references to regulatory compliance and cost management.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management identified macroeconomic uncertainty, regulatory interventions, and industry-wide moderation in volume growth as primary risks.

  • Mitigation strategies include investing in automation, enhancing last-mile capabilities, and maintaining a balanced approach to cost and service quality.

  • Analysts raised concerns about the sustainability of cost reductions, competitive dynamics, and the effect of anti-involution policies on pricing and volume.

Final Takeaway

ZTO Express adjusted its annual parcel volume guidance downward in response to industry moderation and regulatory changes, while management remains focused on high-quality service, cost efficiency, and technological advancement to preserve its leadership position. The company is proactively adapting to a shifting competitive landscape and remains confident in its long-term growth prospects despite near-term challenges.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: China maintains key loan prime rates for straight 6th month amid cautious policy stance
data.description:
China's central bank opted to keep its key lending benchmarks unchanged for a sixth consecutive month in November, in line with market expectations, reinforcing expectations of a cautious monetary policy approach. The one-year Loan Prime Rate ((LPR)), which serves as the benchmark for most corporate and household loans, was maintained at 3.0%.

Similarly, the five-year LPR, the primary reference rate for mortgages, remained steady at 3.5%, following its last adjustment (a 10 basis point cut) in May 2025.

This decision follows the central bank's earlier move this month to leave the seven-day reverse repo rate unchanged, further signaling a preference for stability over aggressive easing.

Investors now turn their attention to next week’s PMI releases for additional insight into the country’s economic health.

On Thursday, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.2% to above 3,950 while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.15% to 13,060 on Thursday, with mainland stocks showing mixed performances after China’s central bank kept key lending rates unchanged as expected, signaling no immediate policy easing, and the offshore yuan steadied around 7.11 per dollar on Thursday.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSE:TDF), (NYSEARCA:CHIQ), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:KBA), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:CWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:KURE).

Currency: (CNY:USD)

More on China markets and economy

  • China's 1.1% Q3 GDP beats forecasts on policy boost, but trade war looms

  • PBoC holds key lending rates steady for fifth straight month

  • U.S.-China trade agreement leads to lifted chip design software restrictions

  • China's trade surplus widens amid weaker exports and deeper import slump

  • China’s May CPI declines fourth consecutive month, PPI deflation eases to two-year low

data.title: Nvidia leads semiconductor stocks higher
data.description:
Shares of Nvidia rose 5% after hours on Wednesday following its latest earnings results. The semiconductor giant posted third-quarter results and guidance that were above Wall Street's forecast amid insatiable demand for artificial intelligence processors.    Several tech stocks are higher in response to the Nvidia results. Micron (MU) +4.3%, AMD +4.5%, Marvell Technology (MRVL) +3.6%, Broadcom (AVGO) +2.9%, Palantir (PLTR) +4%. First Solar (FSLR) +2.4%, Intel (INTC) +2%, Microchip Technology (MCHP) +2%, Monolithic Power Systems (MPWR) +2.3%, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) +2.7%.

The results from the semiconductor giant defy the AI valuation concern raised after Palantir reported its earnings results. Wall Street recently saw a tech selloff, driven in part by a steep drop in Palantir’s shares after its earnings report highlighted stock valuation concern.

data.title: Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Employment Situation 

The long-delayed September jobs report, originally scheduled for October 3, will report today.  The employment situation is a set of monthly labor market indicators based on two separate reports: the establishment survey and the household survey.  Payrolls expected to be up 50K in September from 22K in August. The jobless rate is expected to be steady at 4.3 percent.

8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time. 

8:30 AM Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index  The general conditions index from this business outlook survey is a diffusion index of manufacturing conditions within the Philadelphia Federal Reserve district.  Growth is seen flat in November from -12.8 in October.

8:45 AM Fed's Hammack Speech 

Cleveland Federal Reserve Bank President Beth Hammack gives opening remarks before the"2025 Financial Stability Conference: Financial Stability in a Time of Rapid Economic and Technological Change" at the Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland.

10:00 AM Existing Home Sales  Existing home sales tally the number of previously constructed homes, condominiums, and co-ops in which a sale closed during the month.  Sales are expected to gain to 4.09 million rate in October versus 4.06 million in September.

10:00 AM Leading Indicators  The index of leading economic indicators is a composite of 10 forward-looking components, including building permits, new factory orders, and unemployment claims. The index is seen down 0.3 percent in October.

10:00 AM Quarterly Services Survey  The Census Bureau quarterly services survey focuses on information and technology-related service industries. These include information; professional, scientific and technical services; administrative & support services; and waste management and remediation services.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report  The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country. The level of inventories helps determine prices for natural gas products. 

11:00 AM Kansas City Fed Manufacturing Index  The Kansas City Fed index offers a monthly assessment of change in the region's manufacturing sector. Positive readings indicate monthly growth, and negative readings indicate monthly contraction.

1:40 PM Fed's Goolsbee Speech

Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee participates in moderated discussion before the CFA Society of Indianapolis 2025 Annual Lunch & New Charterholder Ceremony.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet  The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.

data.title: Williams-Sonoma raises operating margin outlook to 17.8%-18.1% as AI, retail investments drive Q4 optimism
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Williams-Sonoma, Inc. (WSM) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Laura Alber reported "strong results in the third quarter of 2025 with an accelerating positive top line comp and continued outperformance in our profitability." Alber highlighted a 4% comparable sales increase, positive comps across all brands, and a 17% operating margin with $1.96 earnings per share, up 5% year-over-year. The company reiterated full-year comparable brand revenue growth guidance of 2% to 5% and raised operating margin guidance by 40 basis points to 17.8%-18.1%.
  • Alber emphasized ongoing market share gains, noting "our continued strong results reflect the power of our operating model, industry-leading channel experiences and strong portfolio of brands." She pointed to an 8.5% comp in retail for Q3 and successful openings of 14 newly remodeled or repositioned stores. AI initiatives were cited as key drivers, with over 60% of customer chats now resolved without human assistance and the launch of Olive, an AI shopping companion.
  • Alber described the company’s 6-point tariff mitigation plan and detailed progress in brands: Pottery Barn posted a 1.3% comp, West Elm 3.3%, Williams-Sonoma 7.3%, and Pottery Barn Kids 4.4%. B2B business grew 9%, and emerging brands like Rejuvenation, Mark and Graham, and GreenRow delivered double-digit comp growth. Alber stated, "We see tremendous opportunity to continue to lead our industry as we execute on our vision to own the home and the places where our customers work, stay and play."
  • CFO Jeff Howie stated, "We delivered positive comps for the fourth straight quarter. Furniture and nonfurniture categories both ran positive comps, reflecting strength across all categories of our offering." Howie underscored supply chain improvements, higher merchandise margins, and operating margin expansion, while noting continued tariff headwinds. He reported $1.88 billion in Q3 net revenue and a "cash balance of $885 million with no outstanding debt."

Outlook

  • The company reiterated its fiscal year 2025 net revenue guidance, expecting full-year comps in the range of 2% to 5% and total net revenues up 0.5% to 3.5%, with operating margin guidance raised to 17.8%-18.1%. Guidance assumes "no meaningful changes in the macroeconomic environment or interest rates or housing turnover."
  • Howie indicated, "Our higher operating margin guide reflects both the strong results we have delivered year-to-date and the expectation that tariffs will have a greater impact on our margins in Q4."

Financial Results

  • Williams-Sonoma reported Q3 net revenue of $1.88 billion and a 4% comp, with all brands and channels posting positive comps. Retail comps rose 8.5% and e-commerce 1.9%.
  • Gross margin reached 46.1%, up 70 basis points from last year, supported by merchandise margin improvement and supply chain efficiencies. SG&A was 29.1% of revenues, up 60 basis points, reflecting higher incentive compensation and increased digital advertising spend.
  • Operating margin was 17%, and diluted EPS grew to $1.96, up 5% year-over-year. The company generated $316 million in operating cash flow, invested $68 million in capital expenditures, and returned $347 million to shareholders through buybacks and dividends.

Q&A

  • Maksim Rakhlenko, TD Cowen: Asked about pricing elasticity and the impact of tariffs in coming quarters. Alber responded, "there's not one quick answer to elasticity because in some [cases], there's room to take up prices. In other cases, you need to take down prices based on what the market is doing."
  • Zachary Fadem, Wells Fargo: Inquired about category performance improvement and macro pressures. Alber noted, "to be able to improve our furniture comps without a significant improvement in housing is a really, really strong sign."
  • Cristina Fernandez, Telsey Advisory: Questioned wide Q4 revenue guidance. Alber explained the range is due to less promotional activity and strong performance last year.
  • Peter Benedict, Baird: Asked about margin sustainability amid tariffs and unit growth prospects. Howie said, "the tariff landscape has been incredibly volatile...the ultimate amount depends upon where the tariffs ultimately land."
  • Christopher Horvers, JPMorgan: Probed growth rate sustainability and margin drivers. Alber said, "there's still room...we're really optimistic about our AZ DC, which is our new DC that came up last year."
  • Jonathan Matuszewski, Jefferies: Asked about targeting higher-income consumers. Alber responded, "we love all our customers...we haven't covered the real super high end at scale yet."
  • Simeon Gutman, Morgan Stanley: Sought clarity on tariff impact timing. Alber emphasized, "the amount that they hit us in Q4 is sizably different than it was in Q3."
  • Steven Zaccone, Citigroup: Asked about Pottery Barn’s relative lag. Alber said, "Pottery Barn's furniture has improved this year, especially on the multiyear stack."
  • Charles Grom, Gordon Haskett: Inquired about category growth sustainability. Alber remained "very optimistic about housing next year."
  • Michael Lasser, UBS: Queried on promotion reduction and margin benefits. Alber stated, "key strategies for our company continue to work...return to growth."
  • Oliver Wintermantel, Evercore ISI: Focused on SG&A moving parts into Q4. Howie highlighted, "higher employment expense was really almost entirely attributable to higher incentive compensation."
  • Sameer Hassan, Chief Technology & Digital Officer: Reported "reduced payroll costs where automation absorbs -- AI automation absorbs repeatable work, reduce vendor costs when we streamline external spend."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, repeatedly challenging management on tariffs, margin sustainability, and competitive dynamics. There was a slightly negative to neutral tone, with several questions probing downside risk and elasticity.
  • Management maintained a confident and slightly defensive tone, particularly on tariff impacts. Alber and Howie consistently referenced mitigation strategies, guidance ranges, and strong execution. Phrases such as "we are confident" and "we have done a really great job of offsetting [tariffs]" were repeated.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone remained confident but more focused on risk mitigation, while analysts demonstrated increased skepticism about tariff headwinds and margin durability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management raised operating margin guidance to 17.8%-18.1% in Q3 2025 from 17.4%-17.8% in Q2 2025, while revenue growth guidance was reiterated. The prior quarter saw a stronger EPS increase and gross margin improvement, while Q3 saw increased caution regarding tariffs.
  • Strategic focus shifted from primarily growth and customer experience in Q2 to a sharper emphasis on cost controls, AI-driven productivity, and tariff mitigation in Q3.
  • Analysts' questions in Q3 concentrated more on tariffs, margin sustainability, and competitive positioning than in Q2, reflecting elevated concerns about future risks.
  • Management’s confidence in AI and supply chain initiatives increased, explicitly linking them to SG&A reductions and customer service improvements.

Risks and Concerns

  • Ongoing and escalating tariffs were cited as a significant headwind, with management noting "the tariff landscape has been incredibly volatile."
  • Lack of housing market recovery remains a challenge, as stated by Alber: "We have not seen a housing recovery."
  • Analyst concerns included the sustainability of margin gains, elasticity of demand with price increases, and the potential for competitive promotional activity.
  • Management outlined a 6-point tariff mitigation plan and highlighted AI-driven cost reductions as key strategies to address these risks.

Final Takeaway

Williams-Sonoma delivered strong Q3 results with robust comps, margin expansion, and raised operating margin guidance, driven by disciplined cost control, strategic investments in AI, and continued market share gains despite tariff headwinds and a challenging macro backdrop. Management reiterated confidence in their multi-brand strategy, supply chain improvements, and AI initiatives to drive both top-line growth and efficiency, positioning the company for a strong close to fiscal 2025 and long-term industry leadership.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: NVIDIA targets $65B Q4 revenue and $0.5T Blackwell & Rubin sales through 2026 while expanding AI infrastructure partnerships
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) Q3 2026

Management View

  • Colette Kress, Executive VP & CFO, reported "revenue of $57 billion, up 62% and a record sequential revenue growth of $10 billion or 22%." Kress stated that "we currently have visibility to $0.5 trillion in Blackwell and Rubin revenue from the start of this year through the end of calendar year 2026." She emphasized that "demand for AI infrastructure continues to exceed our expectations. The clouds are sold out and our GPU installed base, both new and previous generations, including Blackwell, Hopper and Ampere is fully utilized."
  • Kress noted, "Record Q3 data center revenue of $51.2 billion increased 66% year-over-year." She highlighted that NVIDIA's networking business "generated revenue of $8.2 billion, up 162% year-over-year."
  • Kress detailed new and expanding partnerships: "AWS and HUMAIN expanded their partnership, including the deployment of up to 150,000 AI accelerators, including our GB300," and "xAI and HUMAIN also announced a partnership in which the 2 will jointly develop a network of world-class GPU data centers anchored by the flagship 500-megawatt facility."
  • She said, "Blackwell gained further momentum in Q3, as GB300 crossed over GB200 and contributed roughly 2/3 of the total Blackwell revenue."
  • On China, Kress explained, "H20 sales were approximately $50 million, sizable purchase orders never materialized in the quarter due to geopolitical issues and the increasingly competitive market in China."
  • On new products, Kress stated, "The Rubin platform is on track to ramp in the second half of 2026. Powered by 7 chips, the Vera Rubin platform will once again deliver an x-factor improvement in performance relative to Blackwell."
  • Jen-Hsun Huang, CEO, commented, "As a reminder, NVIDIA is unlike any other accelerator. We excel at every phase of AI from pre-training and post training to inference."
  • Huang said, "The world is undergoing 3 massive platform shifts at once... NVIDIA is uniquely addressing each of the 3 transformations."

Outlook

  • Kress provided, "Total revenue is expected to be $65 billion, plus or minus 2%. At the midpoint, our outlook implies 14% sequential growth driven by continued momentum in the Blackwell architecture."
  • Kress stated, "GAAP and non-GAAP gross margins are expected to be 74.8% and 75%, respectively, plus or minus 50 basis points."
  • She added, "Looking ahead to fiscal year 2027, and input costs are on the rise, but we are working to hold gross margins in the mid-70s."
  • Kress reiterated, "Consistent with last quarter, we are not assuming any data center compute revenue from China."

Financial Results

  • Kress reported, "GAAP gross margins were 73.4% and non-GAAP gross margins was 73.6%, exceeding our outlook."
  • She noted, "GAAP operating expenses were up 8% sequentially and up 11% on non-GAAP basis."
  • On the balance sheet, "inventory grew 32% quarter-over-quarter, while supply commitments increased 63% sequentially."
  • Gaming revenue was stated as "$4.3 billion, up 30% year-on-year."
  • Professional Visualization revenue reached "$760 million, up 56% year-over-year."
  • Automotive revenue recorded "$592 million, up 32% year-over-year."

Q&A

  • Joseph Moore, Morgan Stanley: "I wonder if you could update us, you talked about the $500 billion of revenue for Blackwell plus Rubin in '25 and '26... are those still kind of the general parameters... any possibility of upside to those numbers as we move forward?" Colette Kress: "Yes, that's correct. We are working into our $500 billion forecast. And we are on track for that... there's definitely an opportunity for us to have more on top of the $500 billion that we announced."
  • Christopher Muse, Cantor Fitzgerald: "Do you see a realistic path for supply to catch up with demand over the next 12 to 18 months?" Jen-Hsun Huang: "We've done a really good job planning our supply chain... all of these exponentials, not to mention just today, I was reading a text from Denis. And he was saying that pre-training and post training are fully intact."
  • Vivek Arya, BofA Securities: "What assumptions are you making on NVIDIA content per gigawatt in that $500 billion number?..." Jen-Hsun Huang: "Hopper generation was probably something along the lines of 20-some-odd, 20 to 25. Blackwell generation, Grace Blackwell particularly is probably 30 to 30 to say, 30 plus or minus and then Rubin is probably higher than that."
  • Benjamin Reitzes, Melius: "What are your plans for that cash? How much goes to buyback versus investing in the ecosystem?..." Jen-Hsun Huang: "We're going to continue to do stock buybacks. We're going to continue to do that. But with respect to the investments, this is really, really important work that we do. All of the investments that we've done so far, all the period, is associated with expanding the reach of CUDA expanding the ecosystem."
  • James Schneider, Goldman Sachs: "You've talked about roughly 40% of your shipments tied to AI inference. I'm wondering, as you look forward into next year, where do you expect that percentage could go...?" Jen-Hsun Huang: "I think that it's hard to know exactly what the percentage of it will be at any given point in time and who. But of course, our hope is that inference is a very large part of the market because if inference is large, then what it suggests is that people are using it in more applications and they're using it more frequently."
  • Timothy Arcuri, UBS: "What's the single biggest bottleneck that worries you that could constrain your growth?" Jen-Hsun Huang: "These are all issues and they're all constraints... But the most important thing that we have to do is do a good job planning... At this point, I'm very confident that NVIDIA's architecture is the best performance per TCO, it is the best performance per watt."
  • Stacy Rasgon, Bernstein: "You said for next year, you're working to hold them in the mid-70s. So I guess, first of all, what are the biggest cost increases?..." Colette Kress: "Next year, there are input prices that are well known in the industries that we need to work through... we do believe as we look at working again on cost improvement, cycle time and mix that we will work to try and hold at our gross margins in the mid-70s."
  • Aaron Rakers, Wells Fargo: "Have you seen, I think you've been fairly adamant in the past that some of these programs never really see deployments. But I'm curious if we're at a point where maybe that's even changed more in favor of just GPU architecture." Jen-Hsun Huang: "So first of all, you're not competing against teams -- excuse me, against a company, you're competing against teams... We're now the only architecture in the world that runs every AI model..."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on revenue targets, supply-demand dynamics, market share assumptions, and strategic capital allocation. Their tone was generally neutral to slightly positive, with persistent curiosity about upside drivers and risk factors.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone throughout, repeatedly expressing certainty in NVIDIA's product leadership and market opportunity. Huang's use of phrases like "I'm very confident" and "we are on track" signals strong conviction.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management sustained a positive outlook, but current management responses revealed increased emphasis on scale, supply chain stability, and new partnerships. There was little sign of defensiveness or hesitation.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Q4 revenue increased from the previous quarter's $54 billion to $65 billion, reflecting ongoing demand acceleration.
  • Gross margin guidance moved up from 73.3%-73.5% previously to 74.8%-75.0% for the coming quarter.
  • Management continues to highlight rapid expansion in the data center and AI infrastructure space, but now places even greater focus on strategic partnerships and ecosystem investments.
  • Analysts' questions in both quarters centered on revenue growth, product cadence, and capital allocation; this quarter, they also probed deeper into supply-demand constraints and the magnitude of ecosystem investments.
  • Management's confidence in their ability to scale and maintain leadership remains high, with more explicit forward targets and visibility to $0.5 trillion in Blackwell and Rubin revenue.

Risks and Concerns

  • Kress cited "geopolitical issues and the increasingly competitive market in China" as a key factor impacting H20 sales, with sizable purchase orders not materializing.
  • Management noted rising input costs and the need for continued cost improvements to maintain gross margins in the mid-70s.
  • Analysts raised concerns about supply catching up with demand, potential bottlenecks (including power, financing, memory, and foundry), and the sustainability of current growth rates.
  • Management stressed ongoing engagement with U.S. and China governments and highlighted global supply chain resiliency efforts.

Final Takeaway

NVIDIA management emphasized another record quarter with strong sequential and annual growth, propelled by robust AI infrastructure demand and leadership in accelerated computing. The company maintained visibility to $0.5 trillion in Blackwell and Rubin revenue through the end of 2026, raised guidance for the next quarter, and described deepening partnerships and ecosystem investments. While highlighting new product ramps and ongoing supply chain initiatives, management acknowledged geopolitical and cost headwinds, but expressed continued confidence in their platform's performance, scale, and long-term opportunity.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript
